questions.question: What is the primary function of the `npx prisma migrate dev` command?
options: It resets the SQL database globally

It connects to your local database, detects any un-tracked changes made inside your `schema.prisma` file, generates raw `.sql` migration files to apply those changes, and ultimately regenerates the TypeScript `@prisma/client` natively
It deploys the Prisma Studio dashboard to an AWS server instance
It converts raw MongoDB syntax into PostgreSQL natively
questions.answer: It connects to your local database, detects any un-tracked changes made inside your `schema.prisma` file, generates raw `.sql` migration files to apply those changes, and ultimately regenerates the TypeScript `@prisma/client` natively
questions.explanation: This command creates an automated workflow. You type `model Post { id Int }`, run the command, and it writes the `CREATE TABLE` script, executes it locally, and updates your VSCode autocomplete instantly.

questions.question: What is 'Database Drift' in the context of Prisma?
options: When developers transition from PostgreSQL to MongoDB natively
When the application's read speeds gradually slow down due to missing B-Tree indexes

When the actual physical structure of the SQL database (e.g. columns, tables) no longer natively matches the schema expected by the Prisma migration history files, usually caused by manual GUI modifications
When Prisma fails to install via NPM locally
questions.answer: When the actual physical structure of the SQL database (e.g. columns, tables) no longer natively matches the schema expected by the Prisma migration history files, usually caused by manual GUI modifications
questions.explanation: If the database expects a `name` column, but a developer manually logs into pgAdmin and deletes the column without using a Prisma migration, Prisma will detect a drift mismatch the next time it runs.

questions.question: Why is it strictly forbidden to execute `npx prisma migrate dev` on a live Production server environment?
options: Because `dev` mathematically requires an active MongoDB replica set natively

Because `dev` is a highly destructive, interactive command designed to aggressively enforce schema parity; if it detects drift, it may automatically drop columns or reset (delete) the entire live customer database to force synchronization natively
Because `dev` blocks the main Node.js event loop causing infinite DDOS latency
Because `dev` deletes all migration files
questions.answer: Because `dev` is a highly destructive, interactive command designed to aggressively enforce schema parity; if it detects drift, it may automatically drop columns or reset (delete) the entire live customer database to force synchronization natively
questions.explanation: Production databases must be treated with infinite caution. `migrate deploy` is designed to strictly read raw SQL scripts and execute them blindly. `migrate dev` is a local tool designed to violently fix development bugs.

questions.question: What is the strict primary purpose of the `npx prisma migrate deploy` command executed within a CI/CD pipeline (like Vercel or GitHub Actions)?
options: To compile TypeScript files into JavaScript globally

To safely check the database's `_prisma_migrations` history table, apply any absolutely new `.sql` migrations that have not yet been executed in production, and then cleanly exit without modifying existing data or generating new schema files natively
To forcibly overwrite the production database with local laptop data
To generate seed records
questions.answer: To safely check the database's `_prisma_migrations` history table, apply any absolutely new `.sql` migrations that have not yet been executed in production, and then cleanly exit without modifying existing data or generating new schema files natively
questions.explanation: When deploying, your codebase has updated. `Deploy` runs the raw SQL scripts attached to that code block against the database before starting the Node server, ensuring the tables exist securely before traffic hits the API.

questions.question: What specifically does the `npx prisma migrate reset` command do in a local development environment?
options: It reverts the last 5 commits strictly

It drops the entire local database completely destroying all data, perfectly recreates the schema purely from the chronological migration files, and then executes the `seed.ts` script to generate fresh, pristine dummy development data natively
It resets the user passwords
It rolls back the Prisma npm package to v3 natively
questions.answer: It drops the entire local database completely destroying all data, perfectly recreates the schema purely from the chronological migration files, and then executes the `seed.ts` script to generate fresh, pristine dummy development data natively
questions.explanation: When your local database is completely broken and drifting, you execute `reset` to wipe the slate clean and start fresh in seconds. This is exceptionally fast locally, which is why seeding logic is natively integrated.
